title: What is Cybersecurity?
description: Define the field and the core goal of protecting data and systems.

slides.title: The Digital Frontier 🏔️
slides.content:
# Welcome to Cybersecurity

Imagine you own a massive, incredibly valuable vault. Not made of steel, but made of **data**.

- Inside is your email.
- Inside are your family photos.
- Inside are your bank passwords.
- Inside is your company's secret recipe for the best coffee in the world.

**Cybersecurity** is the practice of protecting that vault—and the computers and networks it lives on—from thieves, vandals, and spies.

It's not just about stopping hackers in hoodies in a dark room (though that's part of it). It's about ensuring you can always get to your data, that it hasn't been changed, and that no one else can see it unless you want them to.

quizData.question: Based on the intro, is cybersecurity ONLY about stopping hackers?

options.text: Yes, that is the only job.
No, it's also about ensuring data is accessible and unchanged.
Only if you work for a bank.

quizData.explanation: Correct! Cybersecurity protects the **CIA Triad** (we'll learn that soon). It's about availability and integrity too!

slides.title: The Connection to Networking 🌐
slides.content:
# Why are we learning about Networks in a Cyber course?

Simple: **The vault is useless if the roads leading to it are wide open.**

A computer sitting on a desk with no internet is pretty hard to hack remotely. But that's not how the world works.

*   **The Network is the Highway.** Data travels on it.
*   **Cybersecurity is the Border Patrol, the Traffic Cops, and the Armored Car.**

Every single cyber attack you hear about in the news—ransomware, data breaches, identity theft—travels over a **network**. To defend the vault, you must understand the roads. You must know what normal traffic looks like so you can spot the tank full of bad guys trying to sneak in.

slides.title: Pro Tip: Think Like a Defender 🚀
slides.content:
## The Defender Mindset

Throughout this course, we will **not** be teaching you how to hack (well, maybe a little bit of *ethical* hacking to understand the enemy). We are teaching you how to **architect, monitor, and defend**.

> *"To know your Enemy, you must become your Enemy."* - Sun Tzu (Art of War)

We learn the protocols so we can spot when they are being abused.
